title: Our Course Project
text:
I’m honored to be a member of the [group name] project team.
Below, you’ll find a brief summary of our project. To access a detailed project description, please go to https://[your-project-url].
Summary
[provide a biref summary of your project]



 Back to top

title: Welcome to My Analytics Lab
text:
Hello! My name is Azra Azizoğlu.
I am an Industrial Engineering student at Hacettepe University. This is my personal analytics lab where I share my journey through data science, my course projects, and my professional insights.
Please stay tuned to follow my works on data analytics, blog posts, and more.



 Back to top

title: About Me
text: B.S., Industrial Engineering, Hacettepe University, Turkey, 2021 - ongoing.

title: About Me
text:
Internships

Zorlu Energy, Business Development and Investment Intern, 2025
Transformed raw market and operational data into actionable business insights using Excel-based optimization models.
Developed intuitive dashboards to track Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) and visualize complex trends.
Collaborated with management to define success metrics for new projects.
Yepsan Defence and Aviation Industry Inc., Production Planning and Control Intern, 2024
Audited and cleaned OEE (Overall Equipment Effectiveness) data within the ERP system to ensure high data quality.
Analyzed workflow data to identify bottlenecks and improve operational efficiency.

The video offers an extremely interesting view by first discussing what data science is not and clearing up some common misconceptions about the field. It highlights that data science is actually an old practice and not just a recent technical trend. The video is supported by various historical examples from public health cases in the 19th century to weather models from the early 20th century, showing how data has long been used to solve complex problems and disprove old theories. The speaker also discusses how the typical workflow from problem definition to exploratory analysis demonstrates how the field has developed into the predictive and prescriptive systems used today. The video demonstrates that the primary purpose of data science has always been to turn raw information into valuable insights to help with decision-making in any field.



Question 1 (Open-ended): According to the data science workflow and the historical examples discussed in the video, why is “Problem Definition” considered the most critical starting point, and how does it relate to the final “Production” stage?
Answer: The workflow emphasizes that before collecting data or building models, one must clearly define the problem and understand the business value it aims to create. As seen in historical cases like John Snow’s mapping or early weather forecasting, the goal was always to solve a specific real-world challenge. This initial definition is crucial because the final “Production” stage is only successful if the model provides actionable insights that directly address that original problem in a live environment.
Question 2 (Multiple Choice): Based on the lecture slides regarding the definition and common misconceptions of Data Science, which of the following statements is correct?
A) Data Science is interchangeable with AI/ML.
B) Data Science is all about big data.
C) Data Science is the interdisciplinary field that bridges multiple disciplines, including statistics, computer science, domain expertise, and engineering.
D) Data Science doesn’t need domain expertise.
Answer: C

title: Assignment 1
text: The video offers an extremely interesting view by first discussing what data science is not and clearing up some common misconceptions about the field. It highlights that data science is actually an old practice and not just a recent technical trend. The video is supported by various historical examples from public health cases in the 19th century to weather models from the early 20th century, showing how data has long been used to solve complex problems and disprove old theories. The speaker also discusses how the typical workflow from problem definition to exploratory analysis demonstrates how the field has developed into the predictive and prescriptive systems used today. The video demonstrates that the primary purpose of data science has always been to turn raw information into valuable insights to help with decision-making in any field.

title: Assignment 1
text:
Question 1 (Open-ended): According to the data science workflow and the historical examples discussed in the video, why is “Problem Definition” considered the most critical starting point, and how does it relate to the final “Production” stage?
Answer: The workflow emphasizes that before collecting data or building models, one must clearly define the problem and understand the business value it aims to create. As seen in historical cases like John Snow’s mapping or early weather forecasting, the goal was always to solve a specific real-world challenge. This initial definition is crucial because the final “Production” stage is only successful if the model provides actionable insights that directly address that original problem in a live environment.
Question 2 (Multiple Choice): Based on the lecture slides regarding the definition and common misconceptions of Data Science, which of the following statements is correct?
A) Data Science is interchangeable with AI/ML.
B) Data Science is all about big data.
C) Data Science is the interdisciplinary field that bridges multiple disciplines, including statistics, computer science, domain expertise, and engineering.
D) Data Science doesn’t need domain expertise.
Answer: C

The video offers an extremely interesting view by first discussing what data science is not and clearing up some common misconceptions about the field. It highlights that data science is actually an old practice and not just a recent technical trend. The video is supported by various historical examples from public health cases in the 19th century to weather models from the early 20th century, showing how data has long been used to solve complex problems and disprove old theories. The speaker also discusses how the typical workflow from problem definition to exploratory analysis demonstrates how the field has developed into the predictive and prescriptive systems used today. The video demonstrates that the primary purpose of data science has always been to turn raw information into valuable insights to help with decision-making in any field.



Question 1 (Open-ended): According to the data science workflow and the historical examples discussed in the video, why is “Problem Definition” considered the most critical starting point, and how does it relate to the final “Production” stage?
Answer: The workflow emphasizes that before collecting data or building models, one must clearly define the problem and understand the business value it aims to create. As seen in historical cases like John Snow’s mapping or early weather forecasting, the goal was always to solve a specific real-world challenge. This initial definition is crucial because the final “Production” stage is only successful if the model provides actionable insights that directly address that original problem in a live environment.
Question 2 (Multiple Choice): Based on the lecture slides regarding the definition and common misconceptions of Data Science, which of the following statements is correct?
A) Data Science is interchangeable with AI/ML.
B) Data Science is all about big data.
C) Data Science is the interdisciplinary field that bridges multiple disciplines, including statistics, computer science, domain expertise, and engineering.
D) Data Science doesn’t need domain expertise.
Answer: C
